steps.title: Track purpose and method
steps.content: The text first states the study's conclusion: people were more civil after thinking about values. It then explains how the researchers reached that conclusion by comparing an experimental group with a control group.

steps.title: Reject purposes not in the text
steps.content: Choice A says the text suggests design improvements, but it only describes the design. Choice C adds researcher surprise, and choice D adds a widely held belief; neither appears in the passage. The remaining choice matches the conclusion-plus-method structure. The answer is B.
